query: I'm organizing my Claude Code project's memory/config. Where does each of these belong? (a) 'use 2-space indentation and single quotes' — a code-style convention Claude should follow when writing code; (b) our 8-step production deploy procedure; (c) 'NEVER commit .env files' — this must be reliably BLOCKED, not just suggested. For each: name the exact mechanism/location and one-line why. Self-contained question — do not read the filesystem.
baseline_failure: RED: the agent puts all three into CLAUDE.md (including the must-block rule, trusting prose to enforce), and/or refuses (a) claiming code style doesn't belong in CLAUDE.md — a common misconception; the docs explicitly list coding standards as valid CLAUDE.md content.
expected_behavior: (a) CLAUDE.md (or a rules file): stating style conventions is valid memory content
(b) NOT in always-on CLAUDE.md: a multi-step procedure belongs in a skill or a domain rules file loaded when relevant
(c) a hook or permissions deny-rule: CLAUDE.md guides but does not enforce — anything that must be blocked needs settings/hooks, not memory prose
anti_expectation: Does NOT tell the user to re-paste their whole linter config into CLAUDE.md — stating the convention is enough; the linter enforces it
Does NOT put (c) ONLY in a hook while also deleting every mention from memory if the user wants Claude to know the policy — knowing (memory) and enforcing (hook) can coexist

baseline_observed.method: 2 isolated haiku probes from model knowledge, then GREEN with the skill's what-goes-where table in context

runs.note: INCONSISTENT: placement discipline held ((a) CLAUDE.md ok, (c) enforcement not memory) but mechanisms hallucinated — '.claude/settings.json style section', deploy routed to docs/ not a skill
INCONSISTENT: same shape — (c) enforcement instinct correct, but invented 'beforeCommit' hook name and '.claude/agents/deploy.md' as the skill path

baseline_observed.verdict: INCONSISTENT — the discipline (guide vs enforce) does not need the skill on haiku; the exact mechanism names do (settings keys, skill paths, hook names all hallucinated). The skill's value here is factual precision, not discipline — weaker than paths-rule but real.

runs.note: (a) CLAUDE.md, (b) .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, (c) permissions.deny — all three mechanisms exact
